title: Datadog の 404 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately identifies common causes of Datadog 404 errors and provides correct troubleshooting steps and code examples.
Claims are well-supported by official Datadog documentation, API references, and general HTTP standards.
The technical guidance is sound and aligns with best practices for interacting with the Datadog platform.
The information regarding log file paths and API key usage is current and accurate.
The article includes a proper disclaimer, reducing perceived risk.
no URLs resolved to known official domains

sources.title: HTTP 404 - Wikipedia
sources.claim: HTTP 404 error indicates that the server could not provide the requested resource, often associated with 'Not Found' message.

sources.title: HTTP Status Codes: All 63 explained - including FAQ & Video - Umbraco
sources.claim: The 404 Not Found status code means that the server either did not find a current representation for the requested resource or is trying to hide its existence from an unauthorized client.

sources.title: Monitors - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog Monitors API allows management of monitors, implying that specific IDs are required to interact with them.

sources.title: Dashboards - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog Dashboards API allows management of dashboards, implying that specific IDs are required to interact with them.

sources.title: Host Agent Log collection - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog Agent v6 can collect logs and forward them to Datadog from files, the network (TCP or UDP), journald, and Windows channels.

sources.title: Linux - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The default log path for the Datadog Agent on Linux is /var/log/datadog/agent.log.

sources.title: Log analytics and dashboarding in Datadog
sources.claim: Datadog's dashboards make it easy to aggregate, explore, and visualize your log analytics and metrics in graphs.

sources.title: Get all monitors - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The Datadog Monitors API provides an endpoint to get all monitor details, which can be used to confirm existing monitor IDs.

sources.title: Monitors - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The Datadog Monitors API allows fetching details about a specified monitor using its ID.

sources.title: Dashboards - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The Datadog Dashboards API allows fetching details about a specified dashboard using its ID.

sources.title: Monitors - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog API has versioning, and checking for deprecated API versions is a valid troubleshooting step.

sources.title: Deprecation notice for TLS version < 1.2 - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog has deprecation policies for its services and APIs, and older versions may become unsupported, leading to errors.

sources.title: Datadog Python API Docs | dltHub
sources.claim: Datadog requests require an API key (api_key) and an application key (app_key) for authentication, and regional sites may vary (e.g., api.datadoghq.eu).

sources.title: API and Application Keys - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: API keys and application keys are essential for authentication and authorization when interacting with Datadog's APIs.

sources.title: Common Datadog Errors and What to Do About Them - MetricFire
sources.claim: Using an incorrect API key with Datadog can lead to various issues and disruptions, including the inability to authenticate and access your Datadog account.

sources.title: Put request getting 404 status while trying to update datadog dashboards - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: An incorrect dashboard ID in an API request can lead to a 404 error when interacting with Datadog dashboards.

sources.title: HTTP Check - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The Datadog HTTP check can detect bad response codes, such as 404, indicating resource unavailability.

sources.title: Monitors - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog provides various monitor types, including metric-based monitors, which rely on metrics being sent to the platform.

sources.title: Getting Started with Dashboards - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Datadog dashboards allow users to manage and view their dashboards through the UI.

sources.title: Monitors API (v1) - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The Datadog Monitors API provides methods like 'get' that require a monitor ID.

sources.title: Dashboards API (v1) - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: The Datadog Dashboards API provides methods like 'get' that require a dashboard ID.

sources.title: datadogpy/datadog/api/monitors.py at master - GitHub
sources.claim: The `datadog.api.Monitor.get_all()` method in the Datadog Python client library can retrieve all monitor details, including ID and name.

sources.title: [ Datadog ] I want to adjust the Agent's log size and related settings. - CloudZ Support Portal
sources.claim: The default log file path for the Datadog Agent on Windows is C:\ProgramData\Datadog\logs\agent.log.

sources.title: Agent Log Files - Datadog Docs
sources.claim: Checking agent logs for connection errors or issues with metric submission is a standard troubleshooting step for Datadog Agent problems.

sources.title: Datadog Agent Metrics not appearing in Datadog - Stack Diagnosis
sources.claim: If the Datadog Agent is not sending metrics or if there are misconfigurations, metrics will not appear in Datadog.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Integrate the provided source URLs as inline citations within the article text for each factual claim.
Optional improvement: Complete the truncated sentence regarding API version deprecation for clarity and completeness.
Optional improvement: Ensure the Python script for initializing the Datadog API client includes the `initialize` call with both `api_key` and `app_key` for correct functionality.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
